reasoning: As of late May 2025 (post-Monaco GP), Lewis Hamilton (63 points) trails Charles Leclerc (79 points) by 16 points in the F1 Drivers' Championship. While a 16-point gap is not mathematically insurmountable over the remaining ~16 races of a 24-race season, the underlying performance data strongly favors Leclerc. Leclerc leads the qualifying head-to-head 6-1 against Hamilton, with a substantial average pace advantage of ~0.3 seconds. Moreover, Hamilton has reportedly struggled heavily with the handling and setup of the Ferrari SF-25, showing inconsistent race pace compared to Leclerc's "flawless" driving. Bookmakers' head-to-head odds from mid-May price Hamilton at 6/1 against Leclerc's 1/14, pointing to an implied probability for Hamilton in the 13-14% range. Adding in the fact that Leclerc just doubled his points lead over Hamilton at the Monaco GP (from 8 points to 16 points), the probability of Hamilton outscoring Leclerc by season's end should be slightly lower than the mid-May estimates. Expecting Leclerc to continue out-qualifying and out-racing Hamilton barring any massive mid-season breakthrough from Hamilton with new car upgrades.
